overview.brief_summary: A targeted spear-phishing campaign against healthcare employees leads to initial access, followed by exploitation of vulnerabilities using PowerShell and creation of scheduled tasks for persistence.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2014-4123 (Suggested): Microsoft Internet Explorer contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows remote attackers to gain privileges via a crafted web site.
stage.description: The attacker sends a targeted email to healthcare employees containing a link to a malicious website designed to trick them into revealing their login credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2018-8453 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Win32k contains a vulnerability that allows an attacker to escalate privileges.
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell for lateral movement and privilege escalation within the network by executing malicious scripts.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates sensitive patient data for espionage purposes using automated tools.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated attack targeting healthcare employees involves spear-phishing, exploitation of vulnerabilities using PowerShell, and creation of scheduled tasks for persistence, ultimately leading to the exfiltration of sensitive patient data.
